title: National%20Environmental%20Management%20Authority%20MIS%20System%20Casestudy%20May%202017
content:

Case Study  
 
 
Customer  
The National Environment 
Management Authority (NEMA) 
- KENYA  is the principal 
instrument of government in 
the implementation of all 
policies relating to the 
environment  
 
Industry  
Government Agency  
 
Business Situation  
NEMA -Kenya was running 
manual business processes that 
were costly and tedious to 
handle .  
 
Solution  
In an effort to introduce 
efficiency and transparency to 
the existing business processes, 
NEMA implemented the Data 
management information 
system that automates key 
business operations  and serves 
as the central data repository  
 
Benefits  
 Provide e -services t o the public  
 Increased efficiency  
 Easier data management  
 Reduced turnaround time  
 Reduced operational costs  
 
 
 
 
  Improve efficiency and reduce  operational cost  
by a utomat ing key business processes.  
 
Customer Profile  
The National Environment Management Authority (NEMA) - KENYA is 
established under the Environmental Management and Coordination Act 
(EMCA) No. 8 of 1999, as the principal instrument of government in the 
implementation of all policies relating to the environme nt. NEMA range of 
services include Registration of environmental experts, Environmental 
licensing, environmental auditing, Environmental incident management and 
disaster prevention, environmental inspection and reporting and 
Environmental Impact Assessment  (EIA)  
 
Business Situation  
Before implementation of the Environmental Management Information 
System, services offered by NEMA  - KENYA  were manual .  
 This proved to be costly  and tedious.  
 The turnaround time on service delivery was very long  with 
application s taking weeks to be  acted upon . 
 NEMA also lacked a single source  of environmental data.  
 The manual De bt collection/ follow up system could not track partially 
paid licenses nor was it able to track license expiry dates hence  there 
was loss in revenue.  
 It was hard to generate reports  
 There was a lot of record loss  coupled with an inefficient search  that 
made it impossible  to quickly locate information  
 The manual system greatly lacked integrity in the manual process .  
 
Solution  
Techno Brain was selected by NEMA - Kenya to provide a National 
Environmental Management System  that would address the problems stated 
above . The National Environmental Management system is an electronic web 
based solution which has enabled NEMA - Kenya to carry out its mandate with 
added efficiency  and ease. The system provides an e -Services portal that is 
accessible to all citizens  where they access and submit online requests to 
NEMA . It also gives  NEMA a solution to effectively manage their operations  
electronically and enforc e standard  operation procedures .  
 
 
 
 
Environment Data Management Information System  

 
Copyright © 201 4 Techno Brain. All rights reserved.  
www.technobraingroup.com  
Case Study  
Major Features of the Environmental Data Management Information System:  
 User Registration  - allows user to register online.  
 Financial system integration  - System integrates with Financial Management System for license  and pro ject 
application payments verification . 
 Licensing Module  – The system generates and issues  license  certificates  for E nvironment Impact Analysis (EIA) , 
Waste M anagement, Water Quality, Noise, Ozone Depleting Substances (ODS), Bio Diversity  
 Automated workflows  – The system a llows user to submit license application forms online. Module also allows 
NEMA to review and approve  all received application . The system is integrated with email and sms to ensure 
sending of notifications to users.  
 Geographical Information System (GIS) integration  – Provides spatial  information from all sections (O zone 
Depleting Substances , Waste Mana gement, Water Quality, Environment Impact Analysis (EIA)  Inspection) by the 
use of maps with project coordinates (longitude and Latitude)  
 Environmental Impact audit (EIA) module  – Used by users with EIA licenses to submit environmental audit 
reports. NEMA uses this module to review all submitted reports using a system defined review format.  
 Incident Management & Inspections  – Used by users to report environmental incidents that can lead to disasters 
such as Oil spills, illegal constructions, unlicensed dumping, floods, fires, noise nuisance etc.  
 File movement Registry (Record Management ) - used to register files, letters and licenses that are bound for 
internally and externally dispatch.  
 E-Library  - maintains the e -books in the system with the option of reading the books online.  
 Electronic Documen t Management  capabilities  
 Email and SMS Notifications  – Used to send notifications to system users e.g. when license application has been 
approved applicant is notified either by SMS or EMAIL based on selected preference.  Users can also query for their 
application status on USSD.  
 Search Configuration  – Allows users to quickly locate information  
 Reports and Dashboards . –It’s now possible for NEMA to know how many licenses have expired or due to expire, 
the license type and worth.  
 
Technologies:  Microsoft SQL  Server 2008 R2, SharePoint server 2010, Visual Studio 2010, Windows Server 2008 R2  
 
Benefits :  
By deploying a highly customizable solution  Techno Brain was able to effectively map NEMA processes  and procedures.  
The key system benefits include:  
 By automating key business processes, the turnaround time was reduced by 80%. 
 Customer experience was improved . The  general public could access NEMA services from anywhere  without 
having to physically go to the NEMA offices to pick up papers  documents . The system also generates certificates.  
 Internally it  is now easier to manage license application fees that are received by NEMA. The system integrates 
with the financial system, keeps track of license expiry dates and sends notifications for renewal.   
 NEMA i s able to quickly generate reports that aid in quick decision making.
